channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korean authorities seek warrant to detain impeached President Yoon in martial law probe
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean law enforcement officials have requested a court warrant to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol as they investigate whether his short-lived martial law decree this month amounted to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jetliner skids off runway and bursts into flames while landing in South Korea, killing 179
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A jetliner skidded off a runway, slammed into a concrete fence and burst into flames Sunday in South Korea after its landing gear apparently failed to deploy. All but two of the 181 people aboard were killed in one of the country’s worst aviation disasters, officials said.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Political world offers condolences for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Members of the political world offered condolences and warm wishes for the family of former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday afternoon. They honored Carter’s decades of achievement and service to the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Notable quotes by Jimmy Carter
item.description: Some quotations from Jimmy Carter. We have a tendency to exalt ourselves and to dwell on the weaknesses and mistakes of others. I have come to realize that in every person there is something fine and pure and noble, along with a desire for self-fulfillment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, NewsNation

item.title: Former President Jimmy Carter dies at 100
item.description: Former President Carter, the unassuming Georgia peanut farmer whose improbable political rise took him to the governor’s mansion before the White House, died peacefully on Sunday in Plains, Ga., according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Scott Wong, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon men search for Sasquatch, die in Washington forest
item.description: Two men from Portland searching for Sasquatch and missing since early Christmas morning were found dead in the Gifford Pinchot National Forest, likely from exposure and being ill-prepared.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nasty norovirus is back in full force with US cases of the stomach virus surging
item.description: Cases of a wretched stomach bug are surging in parts of the United States this winter, according to government data.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Colorado suspect wanted in alleged failed bank robbery after handing teller note that was illegible
item.description: A Colorado man allegedly failed an attempted bank robbery when he handed a note to a teller who was unable to decipher what he had written on it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 15 hurt in Florida when train hits fire truck that drove onto tracks after another train passed
item.description: Three firefighters and a dozen passengers were injured in Florida on Saturday when a fire truck with its lights flashing drove around rail crossing arms and into the path of a high-speed passenger train after waiting for another train to pass, according to video of the incident and a person briefed
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Serial killer Richard Ramirez’s bride a ‘superfan’ who put herself ‘top of the line’ for his love: pal
item.description: Richard Ramirez, the serial killer who was sentenced to death following his heinous crimes in California, died while on death row in 2013. He was 53.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Mega Millions player in California is $1.22B richer
item.description: After no players won on Christmas Eve, the Mega Millions lottery jackpot ballooned to $1.22 billion. The winning ticket was sold Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Mega Millions winning numbers drawn for $1.2 billion jackpot
item.description: The winning numbers are in for Friday's Mega Millions, with a jackpot that sits at an estimated $1.2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sportscasting icon Greg Gumbel dies at 78
item.description: Sportscaster Greg Gumbel, best known for his long career with CBS Sports, has died Friday, Dec. 27, according to CBS Sports and Gumbel's family.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: US homelessness up double digits, rising numbers of asylum seekers, affordability crisis among causes
item.description: A report from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development shows homelessness rose 18% in 2024, with causes including asylum seekers, lack of affordable housing and natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on a Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: More than a third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in debt this holiday season: report
item.description: (KRON) -- More than one third of Americans racked up over $1,000 in credit card debt this holiday season, according to a new report. The report from LendingTree indicates that 36% of Americans took on an average of $1,181 in debt, up from $1,028 in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News hosts Sean Hannity, Ainsley Earhardt announce engagement
item.description: "We are overjoyed and so thankful to our families for all of their love and support during this wonderful time in our lives," the couple said.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Homelessness up 18% in U.S. amid lack of affordable housing, surge of migrants
item.description: (AP) — The United States saw an 18.1% increase in homelessness this year, a dramatic rise driven mostly by a lack of affordable housing as well as devastating natural disasters and a surge of migrants in several parts of the country, federal officials said Friday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles building from famous rock album cover goes up in flames
item.description: Once featured on the cover of a Doors album, the former site of the Morrison Hotel was destroyed by a fire on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KRON4

item.title: Bald eagle finally becomes official US bird
item.description: The eagle had long been associated with the U.S., but it only just officially became the national bird.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Why this Mexican American woman played a vital role in the US sacramental peyote trade
item.description: MIRANDO CITY, Texas (AP) — The late Amada Cardenas was called many things — the “angel of peyote,” the “peyote rose” or simply “Grandma Amada.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ukrainian refugee finds peace through Tarpon Springs color guard
item.description: The holidays bring a lot of happiness for many people, especially one Tarpon Springs High School student who is thankful she can celebrate in safety here in America.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4
